revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chōshizuka Kofun (Saga)]][[ja:銚子塚古墳 (佐賀市)]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Chōshizuka Kofun | native_name =銚子塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Choshizuka-kofun (Saga) zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Chōshizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Chōshizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33.317869|N|130.299833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Saga|qid=Q209766|1=Saga (city)}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th - 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|4.33|N|130|17|59.4|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} The {{nihongo|'''Chōshizuka Kofun'''|銚子塚古墳|qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kinryū-chō neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Saga, Saga|Saga]], {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1978.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/211440|title=銚子塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Chōshizuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} keyhole-shaped tomb built on flat land at the southern foot of Mt. Kinryū, a peak in the {{ill|Mount Sefuri|en|Mount Sefuri|qid=Q8528436|lt=Sefuri mountain range|1=Mount Sefuri}} in the northern part of the Saga Plain. The tumulus is large, with a total length of 98 meters and is orientated to the west. The surrounding area includes the presumed site of the [[Hizen Kokufu]] ([[Kokufu|Provincial Capital]]) and the remains of [[Hizen Kokubun-ji]] {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|qid=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}}. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters, and the rectangular anterior portion has a maximum width of 32 meters, and a height of 4.8 meters. A moat 16 to 28 meters wide surrounds the entire tumulus, the front part of which partially incorporates the valley topography. The mound is built in two tiers at the front and three at the rear mound, with ''[[fukiishi]]'' visible. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, although several jar-shaped earthenware vessels have been collected on the slope connecting the two portions of the tumulus. These pot-shaped earthenware have a curved double rim, a vertical neck, a spherical body, a pointed base with perforations at the bottom that were made before firing, and are painted vermillion. These are old-style {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery, very similar to that of a jar-shaped ''haniwa'' clay figures.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> This tumulus was constructed in the late 4th to early 5th century, and is the second largest in Saga Prefecture. It has not been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]], so details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] and any surviving {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is uncertain.<ref name="Shiseki"/> The tumulus is 6.5 kilometers north of {{ill|Saga Station|en|Saga Station|qid=Q1042665|lt=Saga Station|1=Saga Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nagasaki Main Line|en|Nagasaki Main Line|qid=Q1078117|lt=Nagasaki Main Line|1=Nagasaki Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links==
{{template-inline|Choshizuka-kofun (Saga)|qq=}} *[https://www.sagabai.com/main/?cont=kanko&fid=530 Saga tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5350 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
